Mr. O'Neil stated that he filed a report on status of administration which has a draft of the accounting attached as exhibit B, that he's still missing some statements from the date of death through October of that year, that he has no picture of what the share of stocks were, and that they are attempting to subpoena the financial institutions for those records because the banks would not provide Mrs. Fernan with the requested statements because more than seven years have passed. The court noted that the accounting was ordered back in November of last year. Mr. O'Neil stated that the court ordered the accounting after Mrs. Nakagawa had died, that John Cuerva's petition for appointment was denied which is when Mrs. Fernan came in, and that Mr. Cuerva served as a special administrator for less than a month and never took possession of any assets. The court referred to Probate Code 10953(c) and directed Mr. O'Neil to file an accounting and then amend it when he receives the records, to file an inventory and appraisal, and to also file something with respect to the time for which Mr. Cueva served as the special administrator. Mr. Rich informed the court that his client will be filing a petition for preliminary distribution. Off Calendar Jan-24-2023 Continued To Apr-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Julie Westerling, Esq. for Minna Fernan (Petitioner). Alexander Rich, Esq. for Nancy Miller (Devisee). James O'Neill, Esq. for Kathy Nakagawa (Deceased Executor).

Mr. O'Neill stated that an administrator needs to be appointed, that the inventory and appraisal is not on file yet, that they need some missing statements, and that there is escheated property which they cannot pursue until someone is appointed. The Court inquired about the progress in the last five months. Mr. O'Neill stated that they hired someone to do the accounting and in order for them to proceed that they need information from the date of death to November 2015.

The Court addressed the petition to appoint Ms. Fernan as successor. Mr. Rich stated that there was no objection to that petition. There being no objection, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. Minna Fernan is appointed with full authority. Bond is set at $305,000.00. Michael Kasolas is appointed the probate referee. Order for the same signed in open court. Status on close of estate is set for February 13, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Mr. O'Neill requested a 90-day continuance, stated that the probate referee will need at least four to six weeks to prepare the inventory and appraisal, and noted that additional time may be needed since the escheated property process can take up to six months.

The Court continued the status on the filing of a petition to approve the accounting to April 18, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cristobal. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Approve The Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-29-2022 Continued To Jan-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil appeared by zoom for John Cuerva (petitioner).
Julie Westerling appeared by zoom for Minna Fernan (proposed administrator).
Alexander Rich appeared by zoom for Nancy Miller (beneficiary).

The court stated that it saw Mr. O'Neil's status report and that it appreciates it having been filed. Mr. O'Neil stated that at the moment the plan is to dismiss John Cuerva's petition and file a petition to have professional fiduciary Minna Fernan appointed, that Mr. Rich's client will nominate Mrs. Fernan, and that if the petition is successful, they will get the information that is needed to file the required documents (accounting/inventory and appraisal). In response to Mrs. Westerling's questions, the court advised her to use the same case number when filing her petition, that the issue of how statutory fees are to be split is not before it right now, that the requirement of a bond will be carried over to her client and the amount of that bond will be determined by the value of the estate, and that Mrs. Fernan will need apply for her own bond. Mr. O'Neil stated that there is a current bond of $305,000.00 which is based on an estate bank account with about $186,000.00, Chevron stocks, dividends which have been escheated to the State, other stock called "cousins" with dividends, and other unclaimed property. Mr. O'Neil also stated that he will be consulting with Mrs. Fernan who although is not present is willing to step up to the position and that they will file a petition for appointment of special administrator. The court informed the parties that it needs to be a petition for general appointment of an administrator. Mr. O'Neil stated that they will file said petition within a week or so and requested that Mr. Cuerva's petition be taken off calendar. Status on filing of a petition to approve the accounting is continued to January 24, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and then will be continued to the same date that is given to Mrs. Fernan's petition. The court stated that it is aware and that it does not expect any progress on the accounting until Mrs. Fernan is appointed. John Cuerva's Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Nov-29-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil, via Zoom, for John Cuerva, Petitioner; Alexander Rich, via Zoom, for and with Nancy Miller, Petitioner.

The court notes of Mr. O'Neil's possible conflict of interest in representing Mr. Cuerva if he is appointed as successor personal representative given that he represented the deceased Executor Kathy Nakagawa. Mr. O'Neli reports of having consent from Ms. Nakagawa's personal Representative. Mr. O'Neil reports of discussing this issue with Mr. Rich and acknowledges the court's concerns regarding the possible conflict of interest issue. Mr. Rich reports of his client's desire to swiftly close this matter and advises that his client does not wish to serve as Administrator. The court advises counsel that this matter may resolve sooner if Mr. Rich's client petitions to be appointed Administrator. The court confirms that Mr. O'Neil is requesting to extend Letters of Special Administration so that his client can continue to obtain bank records and confirms that Mr. Cuerva's law practice includes probate matters. The court advises that it is not inclined to issue extended Letters as it doesn't appear that an accounting is an urgent matter at this time and at least not until an Administrator or Executor is formally appointed by the court. The court advises that it will consider extending the Letters of Special Administration if Mr. Cuerva retains independent counsel.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Nov-29-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
